SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF ARYAN DAVANI IN OPPOSITION TO DECLARATION OF JENNIFER CREECH IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS REPLY TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS (TRANSACTION ID # 62087901) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DAVANI, ARYAN , INDIVIDUALLY
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-04-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUES THIS MATTER TO JUNE 11, 2018 TO GIVE DEFENDANTS AN OPPORTUNITY TO CONSIDER WHETHER THEY WILL AGREE THAT, IF THIS CASE IS LITIGATED IN FLORIDA THAT CALIFORNIA LAW, NOT FLORIDA LAW, WILL GOVERN THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF ARYAN DAVANI AND DEFENDANTS' DEFENSES, INCLUDING CALIFORNIA LAW REGARDING STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND EXCEPTIONS THERETO. IF DEFENDANTS SO AGREE, THEY MUST FILE A DOCUMENT SO STATING BY JUNE 7, 2018. IF DEFENDANTS SO AGREE, THE COURT WILL STAY THIS CASE PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE FLORIDA CASE. ABSENT SUCH AN AGREEMENT, THE MANDATORY FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE IS UNREASONABLE AND UNENFORCEABLE BECAUSE MR. DAVANI WOULD NOT BE ABLE TO LITIGATE HIS CLAIMS IN FLORIDA ON THEIR MERITS. LABOR CODE 925 IS INAPPLICABLE TO THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT BECAUSE THE AGREEMENT WAS EXECUTED BEFORE JANUARY 1, 2017, ITS TERMS PRECLUDE ORAL MODIFICATION, THERE WAS NO CONSIDERATION SUPPORTING THE PURPORTED ORAL MODIFICATION, AND MR. DAVANI NEVER FULFILLED THE TERMS OF THE PURPORTED ORAL MODIFICATION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-09-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JENNIFER CREECH, E-ZASSI, LLC, AND EZASSI, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS TREATED AS A MOTION SEEKING DISMISSAL OR STAY OF THIS CASE AND, SO TREATED, THE REQUEST TO STAY IS GRANTED BASED ON DEFENDANTS' ASSENT THAT, IF THIS CASE IS LITIGATED IN FLORIDA, CALIFORNIA LAW, NOT FLORIDA LAW, WILL GOVERN THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF ARYAN DAVANI CLAIMS AND DEFENDANTS' DEFENSES, INCLUDING CALIFORNIA LAW REGARDING STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND EXCEPTIONS THERETO. THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF PROCEEDINGS IN FLORIDA REGARDING PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS OR A MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY BASED ON THE FLORIDA COURT'S UNWILLINGNESS TO APPLY CALIFORNIA LAW, WHICHEVER OCCURS EARLIER. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTS THE REQUEST OF PLAINTIFF TO REFILE 60 DAYS FROM TODAY AND STAYS THE ENIRE CASE INCLUDING THE ENFORCEMENT OF THE 5 SUBPOENAS. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sam Sani, Esq. 310-935-0405 of Sani Law for Plaintiff Aryan Davani through Court Call.
Joseph Lordan, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant JENNIFER CREECH, E-ZASSI, LLC, EZASSI, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JENNIFER CREECH, E-ZASSI, LLC, EZASSI, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to dismiss for forum non conveniens is treated as a motion seeking dismissal or stay of this case and, so treated, the request to stay is granted based on defendants' assent that, if this case is litigated in Florida, California law, not Florida law, will govern the claims of plaintiff Aryan Davani claims and defendants' defenses, including California law regarding statute of limitations and exceptions thereto. This case is stayed pending the completion of proceedings in Florida regarding plaintiffs' claims or a motion to lift the stay based on the Florida court's unwillingness to apply California law, whichever occurs earlier. Based on defendants' assent that California law governs the claims of plaintiff Aryan Davani claims and defendants' defenses in a Florida court, the mandatory forum selection clause is reasonable and enforceable. Labor Code 925 is inapplicable to the parties' agreement because the agreement was executed before January 1, 2017, its terms preclude oral modification, there was no consideration supporting any purported oral modification, Mr. Davani never fulfilled the terms of any purported oral modification, and there was no written modification. The court finds that Mr. Davani never accepted defendants' offer made on March 7, 2017 to modify the agreement. Mr. Davani's statement that he accepted the offer by sending a fax is inadmissible as against the secondary evidence rule and is not credible in light of the record on this motion.

In addition, the Court grants the request of Plaintiff to refile 60 days from today and stays the enire case including the enforcement of the 5 subpoenas.

Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Jun-11-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants' motion to dismiss for forum non conveniens is continued to June 11, 2018 to give defendants an opportunity to consider whether they will agree that, if this case is litigated in Florida that California law, not Florida law, will govern the claims of plaintiff Aryan Davani and defendants' defenses, including California law regarding statute of limitations and exceptions thereto. If defendants so agree, they must file a document so stating by June 7, 2018. If defendants so agree, the court will stay this case pending the conclusion of the Florida case. Absent such an agreement, the mandatory forum selection clause is unreasonable and unenforceable because Mr. Davani would not be able to litigate his claims in Florida on their merits. Labor Code 925 is inapplicable to the parties' agreement because the agreement was executed before January 1, 2017, its terms preclude oral modification, there was no consideration supporting the purported oral modification, and Mr. Davani never fulfilled the terms of the purported oral modification. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
